metadata.name: neuron-explainer
metadata.summary: A package for neuron activations and explanations

modules.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records
documentation: Utilities for formatting activation records into prompts.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records.calculate_max_activation
documentation: Return the maximum activation value of the neuron across all the activation records.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records.normalize_activations
documentation: Convert raw neuron activations to integers on the range [0, 10].

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records._format_activation_record
documentation: Format neuron activations into a string, suitable for use in prompts.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records.format_activation_records
documentation: Format a list of activation records into a string.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records._format_tokens_for_simulation
documentation:
Format tokens into a string with each token marked as having an "unknown" activation, suitable
for use in prompts.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records.format_sequences_for_simulation
documentation:
Format a list of lists of tokens into a string with each token marked as having an "unknown"
activation, suitable for use in prompts.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activation_records.non_zero_activation_proportion
documentation: Return the proportion of activation values that aren't zero.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.token_connections.load_token_weight_connections_of_neuron
documentation: Load the TokenLookupTableSummaryOfNeuron for the specified neuron.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.token_connections.load_token_lookup_table_connections_of_neuron
documentation: Load the TokenLookupTableSummaryOfNeuron for the specified neuron.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.activations.token_connections.TokenLookupTableSummaryOfNeuron
documentation:
List of tokens and the average activations of a given neuron in response to each
respective token. These are selected from among the tokens in the vocabulary with the
highest average activations across an internet text dataset, with the highest activations
first.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.azure.standardize_azure_url
documentation: Make sure url is converted to url format, not an azure path

modules.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations
documentation: Dataclasses and enums for storing neuron-indexed information about activations. Also, related
helper functions.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations._check_slices
documentation: Assert that the slices are disjoint and fully cover the intended range.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.get_slices_for_splits
documentation:
Get equal-sized interleaved subsets for each of a list of splits, given the number of elements
to include in each split.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.neuron_exists
documentation: Return whether the specified neuron exists.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.load_neuron
documentation: Load the NeuronRecord for the specified neuron.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.load_neuron_async
documentation: Async version of load_neuron.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.read_neuron_file
documentation: Like load_neuron_async, but takes a raw neuron filename.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.get_sorted_neuron_indices
documentation: Returns the indices of all neurons in this layer, in ascending order.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.get_sorted_layers
documentation: Return the indices of all layers in this dataset, in ascending numerical order, as strings.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.ActivationRecord

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.ActivationRecord.tokens
documentation: Tokens in the text sequence, represented as strings.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.ActivationRecord.activations
documentation: Raw activation values for the neuron on each token in the text sequence.
Collated lists of tokens and their activations for a single neuron.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronId

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronId.layer_index
documentation: The index of layer the neuron is in. The first layer used during inference has index 0.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronId.neuron_index
documentation: The neuron's index within in its layer. Indices start from 0 in each layer.
Identifier for a neuron in an artificial neural network.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.ActivationRecordSliceParams

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.ActivationRecordSliceParams.n_examples_per_split
documentation: The number of examples to include in each split.
How to select splits (train, valid, etc.) of activation records.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord

methods.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.max_activation
documentation: Return the maximum activation value over all top-activating activation records.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.train_activation_records
documentation:
Train split, typically used for generating explanations. Consists exclusively of
top-activating records since context window limitations make it difficult to include
random records.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.calibration_activation_records
documentation:
Calibration split, typically used for calibrating neuron simulations. See
http://go/neuron_explanation_methodology for an explanation of calibration. Consists of
top-activating records and random records in a 1:1 ratio.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.valid_activation_records
documentation:
Validation split, typically used for evaluating explanations, either automatically with
simulation + correlation coefficient scoring, or manually by humans. Consists of
top-activating records and random records in a 1:1 ratio.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.test_activation_records
documentation:
Test split, typically used for explanation evaluations that can't use the validation split.
Consists of top-activating records and random records in a 1:1 ratio.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.neuron_id
documentation: Identifier for the neuron.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.random_sample
documentation:
Random activation records for this neuron. The random sample is independent from those used for
other neurons.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.random_sample_by_quantile
documentation:
Random samples of activation records in each of the specified quantiles. None if quantile
tracking is disabled.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.quantile_boundaries
documentation: Boundaries of the quantiles used to generate the random_sample_by_quantile field.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.activations.activations.NeuronRecord.most_positive_activation_records
documentation:
Activation records with the most positive figure of merit value for this neuron over all dataset
examples.
Neuron-indexed activation data, including summary stats and notable activation records.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.fast_dataclasses.test_fast_dataclasses.DataclassC
documentation: Inheritance is a bit tricky with our setup. dataclass_name must be set for instances of these
classes to serialize and deserialize correctly, but if it's given a default value, then subclasses
can't have any fields that don't have default values, because of how constructors are generated
for dataclasses (fields with no default value can't follow those with default values). To work
around this, we set dataclass_name in __post_init__ on the base class, which is called after the
constructor. The implementation does the right thing for both the base class and the subclass.

modules.name: neuron_explainer.fast_dataclasses.fast_dataclasses
documentation: Utilities for dataclasses that are very fast to serialize and deserialize, with limited data
validation. Fields must not be tuples, since they get serialized and then deserialized as lists.
The unit tests for this library show how to use it.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.prompt_builder.PromptFormat

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.prompt_builder.PromptFormat.NONE
documentation: Suitable for use with models that don't use special tokens for instructions.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.prompt_builder.PromptFormat.INSTRUCTION_FOLLOWING
documentation: Suitable for IF models that use <|endofprompt|>.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.prompt_builder.PromptFormat.HARMONY_V4
documentation:
Suitable for Harmony models that use a structured turn-taking role+content format. Generates a
list of HarmonyMessage dicts that can be sent to the /chat/completions endpoint.

Different ways of formatting the components of a prompt into the format accepted by the relevant
API server endpoint.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.prompt_builder.Role
documentation: See https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/chat

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.prompt_builder.PromptBuilder

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.prompt_builder.PromptBuilder.build
documentation:
Validates the messages added so far (reasonable alternation of assistant vs. user, etc.)
and returns either a regular string (maybe with <|endofprompt|> tokens) or a list of
HarmonyMessages suitable for use with the /chat/completions endpoint.

The `allow_extra_system_messages` parameter allows the caller to specify that the prompt
should be allowed to contain system messages after the very first one.
Class for accumulating components of a prompt and then formatting them into an output.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.api_client.exponential_backoff
documentation:
Returns a decorator which retries the wrapped function as long as the specified retry_on
function returns True for the exception, applying exponential backoff with jitter after
failures, up to a retry limit.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.api_client.ApiClient
documentation: Performs inference using the OpenAI API. Supports response caching and concurrency limits.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.token_space_few_shot_examples.Example
documentation:
An example list of tokens as strings corresponding to top token space inputs of a neuron, with a
string explanation of the neuron's behavior on these tokens.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.token_space_few_shot_examples.TokenSpaceFewShotExampleSet

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.token_space_few_shot_examples.TokenSpaceFewShotExampleSet.get_examples
documentation: Returns regular examples for use in a few-shot prompt.
Determines which few-shot examples to use when sampling explanations.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.puzzles.convert_puzzle_to_tokenized_sentences
documentation: Converts a puzzle to a list of tokenized sentences.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.puzzles.convert_puzzle_dict_to_puzzle
documentation: Converts a json dictionary representation of a puzzle to the Puzzle class.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.puzzles.Puzzle
documentation:
A puzzle is a ground truth explanation, a collection of sentences (stored as ActivationRecords) with activations
according to that explanation, and a collection of false explanations

modules.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer
documentation: Uses API calls to generate explanations of neuron behavior.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer._split_numbered_list
documentation: Split a numbered list into a list of strings.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer._remove_final_period
documentation: Strip a final period or period-space from a string.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.NeuronExplainer

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.NeuronExplainer.generate_explanations
documentation: Generate explanations based on subclass-specific input data.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.NeuronExplainer.make_explanation_prompt
documentation:
Create a prompt to send to the API to generate one or more explanations.

A prompt can be a simple string, or a list of HarmonyMessages, depending on the PromptFormat
used by this instance.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.NeuronExplainer.postprocess_explanations
documentation: Postprocess the completions returned by the API into a list of explanations.

Abstract base class for Explainer classes that generate explanations from subclass-specific
input data.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.TokenActivationPairExplainer

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.TokenActivationPairExplainer.postprocess_explanations
documentation: Postprocess the explanations returned by the API
Generate explanations of neuron behavior using a prompt with lists of token/activation pairs.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.TokenSpaceRepresentationExplainer

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.TokenSpaceRepresentationExplainer._add_few_shot_examples
documentation:
Append few-shot examples to the prompt. Each one consists of a comma-delimited list of
tokens and corresponding explanations, as saved in
alignment/neuron_explainer/weight_explainer/token_space_few_shot_examples.py.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explainer.TokenSpaceRepresentationExplainer._add_neuron_specific_prompt
documentation:
Append a neuron-specific prompt to the prompt builder. The prompt consists of a list of
tokens followed by either an explanation (if one is passed, for few shot examples) or by
the beginning of a completion, to be completed by the model with an explanation.

Generate explanations of arbitrary lists of tokens which disproportionately activate a
particular neuron. These lists of tokens can be generated in various ways. As an example, in one
set of experiments, we compute the average activation for each neuron conditional on each token
that appears in an internet text corpus. We then sort the tokens by their average activation,
and show 50 of the top 100 tokens. Other techniques that could be used include taking the top
tokens in the logit lens or tuned lens representations of a neuron.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.scoring.make_explanation_simulator
documentation:
Make a simulator that uses an explanation to predict activations and calibrates it on the given
activation records.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.scoring._simulate_and_score_sequence
documentation: Score an explanation of a neuron by how well it predicts activations on a sentence.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.scoring.aggregate_scored_sequence_simulations
documentation:
Aggregate a list of scored sequence simulations. The logic for doing this is non-trivial for EV
scores, since we want to calculate the correlation over all activations from all sequences at
once rather than simply averaging per-sequence correlations.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.scoring.simulate_and_score
documentation:
Score an explanation of a neuron by how well it predicts activations on the given text
sequences.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.scoring.make_simulator_and_score
documentation: Chain together creating the simulator and using it to score activation records.

modules.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator
documentation:
Code for calibrating simulations of neuron behavior. Calibration refers to a process of mapping from
a space of predicted activation values (e.g. [0, 10]) to the real activation distribution for a
neuron.

See http://go/neuron_explanation_methodology for description of calibration step. Necessary for
simulating neurons in the context of ablate-to-simulation, but can be skipped when using correlation
scoring. (Calibration may still improve quality for scoring, at least for non-linear calibration
methods.)

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.CalibratedNeuronSimulator

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.CalibratedNeuronSimulator.create
documentation:
Create and calibrate a calibrated simulator (so initialization and calibration can be done
in one call).

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.CalibratedNeuronSimulator.calibrate
documentation:
Determine parameters to map from the predicted activation space to the real neuron
activation space, based on a calibration set.

Use when simulated sequences haven't already been produced on the calibration set.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.CalibratedNeuronSimulator.calibrate_from_simulations
documentation:
Determine parameters to map from the predicted activation space to the real neuron
activation space, based on a calibration set.

Use when simulated sequences have already been produced on the calibration set.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.CalibratedNeuronSimulator._calibrate_from_flattened_activations
documentation:
Determine parameters to map from the predicted activation space to the real neuron
activation space, based on a calibration set.

Take numpy arrays of all true activations and all uncalibrated activations on the
calibration set over all sequences.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.CalibratedNeuronSimulator.apply_calibration
documentation: Apply the learned calibration to a sequence of values.

Wrap a NeuronSimulator and calibrate it to map from the predicted activation space to the
actual neuron activation space.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.UncalibratedNeuronSimulator
documentation: Pass through the activations without trying to calibrate.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.LinearCalibratedNeuronSimulator
documentation:
Find a linear mapping from uncalibrated activations to true activations.

Should not change ev_correlation_score because it is invariant to linear transformations.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.calibrated_simulator.PercentileMatchingCalibratedNeuronSimulator
documentation:
Map the nth percentile of the uncalibrated activations to the nth percentile of the true
activations for all n.

This will match the distribution of true activations on the calibration set, but will be
overconfident outside of the calibration set.

modules.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.few_shot_examples
documentation: Few-shot examples for generating and simulating neuron explanations.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.few_shot_examples.Example.first_revealed_activation_indices
documentation:
For each activation record, the index of the first token for which the activation value in the
prompt should be an actual number rather than "unknown".

Examples all start with the activations rendered as "unknown", then transition to revealing
specific normalized activation values. The goal is to lead the model to predict that activation
sequences will eventually transition to predicting specific activation values instead of just
"unknown". This lets us cheat and get predictions of activation values for every token in a
single round of inference by having the activations in the sequence we're predicting always be
"unknown" in the prompt: the model will always think that maybe the next token will be a real
activation.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.few_shot_examples.Example.token_index_to_score
documentation:
If the prompt is used as an example for one-token-at-a-time scoring, this is the index of the
token to score.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.few_shot_examples.FewShotExampleSet

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.few_shot_examples.FewShotExampleSet.get_examples
documentation: Returns regular examples for use in a few-shot prompt.

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.few_shot_examples.FewShotExampleSet.get_single_token_prediction_example
documentation:
Returns an example suitable for use in a subprompt for predicting a single token's
normalized activation, for use with the "one token at a time" scoring approach.
Determines which few-shot examples to use when sampling explanations.

modules.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations
documentation: Dataclasses and enums for storing neuron explanations, their scores, and related data. Also,
related helper functions.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.load_neuron_explanations
documentation: Load scored explanations for the specified neuron.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.load_neuron_explanations_async
documentation: Load scored explanations for the specified neuron, asynchronously.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.read_file
documentation: Read the contents of the given file as a string, asynchronously.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.read_explanation_file
documentation: Load scored explanations from the given filename, asynchronously.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.read_json_file
documentation: Read the contents of the given file as a JSON object, asynchronously.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.get_numerical_subdirs
documentation:
Return the names of all numbered subdirectories in the specified directory.

Used to get all layer directories in an explanation directory.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.get_sorted_neuron_indices_from_explanations
documentation: Return the indices of all neurons in this layer, in ascending order.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ActivationScale

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ActivationScale.NEURON_ACTIVATIONS
documentation: Values represent real activations of the neuron.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ActivationScale.SIMULATED_NORMALIZED_ACTIVATIONS
documentation:
Values represent simulated activations of the neuron, normalized to the range [0, 10]. This
scale is arbitrary and should not be interpreted as a neuron activation.

Which "units" are stored in the expected_activations/distribution_values fields of a
SequenceSimulation.

This enum identifies whether the values represent real activations of the neuron or something
else. Different scales are not necessarily related by a linear transformation.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.SequenceSimulation

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.SequenceSimulation.tokens
documentation: The sequence of tokens that was simulated.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.SequenceSimulation.expected_activations
documentation: Expected value of the possibly-normalized activation for each token in the sequence.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.SequenceSimulation.activation_scale
documentation: What scale is used for values in the expected_activations field.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.SequenceSimulation.distribution_values
documentation:
For each token in the sequence, a list of values from the discrete distribution of activations
produced from simulation. Tokens will be included here if and only if they are in the top K=15
tokens predicted by the simulator, and excluded otherwise.

May be transformed to another unit by calibration. When we simulate a neuron, we produce a
discrete distribution with values in the arbitrary discretized space of the neuron, e.g. 10%
chance of 0, 70% chance of 1, 20% chance of 2. Which we store as distribution_values =
[0, 1, 2], distribution_probabilities = [0.1, 0.7, 0.2]. When we transform the distribution to
the real activation units, we can correspondingly transform the values of this distribution
to get a distribution in the units of the neuron. e.g. if the mapping from the discretized space
to the real activation unit of the neuron is f(x) = x/2, then the distribution becomes 10%
chance of 0, 70% chance of 0.5, 20% chance of 1. Which we store as distribution_values =
[0, 0.5, 1], distribution_probabilities = [0.1, 0.7, 0.2].

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.SequenceSimulation.distribution_probabilities
documentation:
For each token in the sequence, the probability of the corresponding value in
distribution_values.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.SequenceSimulation.uncalibrated_simulation
documentation: The result of the simulation before calibration.
The result of a simulation of neuron activations on one text sequence.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSequenceSimulation

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSequenceSimulation.simulation
documentation: The result of a simulation of neuron activations.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSequenceSimulation.true_activations
documentation: Ground truth activations on the sequence (not normalized)

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSequenceSimulation.ev_correlation_score
documentation:
Correlation coefficient between the expected values of the normalized activations from the
simulation and the unnormalized true activations of the neuron on the text sequence.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSequenceSimulation.rsquared_score
documentation: R^2 of the simulated activations.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSequenceSimulation.absolute_dev_explained_score
documentation:
Score based on absolute difference between real and simulated activations.
absolute_dev_explained_score = 1 - mean(abs(real-predicted))/ mean(abs(real))
SequenceSimulation result with a score (for that sequence only) and ground truth activations.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSimulation

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSimulation.get_preferred_score
documentation:
This method may return None in cases where the score is undefined, for example if the
normalized activations were all zero, yielding a correlation coefficient of NaN.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSimulation.scored_sequence_simulations
documentation: ScoredSequenceSimulation for each sequence

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSimulation.ev_correlation_score
documentation:
Correlation coefficient between the expected values of the normalized activations from the
simulation and the unnormalized true activations on a dataset created from all score_results.
(Note that this is not equivalent to averaging across sequences.)

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSimulation.rsquared_score
documentation: R^2 of the simulated activations.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredSimulation.absolute_dev_explained_score
documentation:
Score based on absolute difference between real and simulated activations.
absolute_dev_explained_score = 1 - mean(abs(real-predicted))/ mean(abs(real)).
Result of scoring a neuron simulation on multiple sequences.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredExplanation

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredExplanation.get_preferred_score
documentation:
This method may return None in cases where the score is undefined, for example if the
normalized activations were all zero, yielding a correlation coefficient of NaN.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredExplanation.explanation
documentation: The explanation used for simulation.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.ScoredExplanation.scored_simulation
documentation: Result of scoring the neuron simulator on multiple sequences.
Simulator parameters and the results of scoring it on multiple sequences

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.explanations.NeuronSimulationResults
documentation: Simulation results and scores for a neuron.

modules.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator
documentation: Uses API calls to simulate neuron activations based on an explanation.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.compute_expected_value
documentation:
Given a map from distribution values (integers on the range [0, 10]) to normalized
probabilities, return an expected value for the distribution.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.parse_top_logprobs
documentation:
Given a map from tokens to logprobs, return a map from distribution values (integers on the
range [0, 10]) to unnormalized probabilities (in the sense that they may not sum to 1).

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.convert_to_byte_array
documentation: Adapted from tether/tether/core/encoder.py.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.handle_byte_encoding
documentation:
Handle the case where the current token is a sequence of bytes. This may involve merging
multiple response tokens into a single token.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.was_token_split
documentation:
Return whether current_token (a token from the subject model) was split into multiple tokens by
the simulator model (as represented by the tokens in response_tokens). start_index is the index
in response_tokens at which to begin looking backward to form a complete token. It is usually
the first token *before* the delimiter that separates the token from the normalized activation,
barring some unusual cases.

This mainly happens if the subject model uses a different tokenizer than the simulator model.
But it can also happen in cases where Unicode characters are split. This function handles both
cases.

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.parse_simulation_response
documentation:
Parse an API response to a simulation prompt.

Args:
    response: response from the API
    prompt_format: how the prompt was formatted
    tokens: list of tokens as strings in the sequence where the neuron is being simulated

functions.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator._parse_no_logprobs_completion
documentation:
Parse a completion into a list of simulated activations. If the model did not faithfully
reproduce the token sequence, return a list of 0s. If the model's activation for a token
is not an integer betwee 0 and 10, substitute 0.

Args:
    completion: completion from the API
    tokens: list of tokens as strings in the sequence where the neuron is being simulated

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.SimulationType

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.SimulationType.ALL_AT_ONCE
documentation:
Use a single prompt with <unknown> tokens; calculate EVs using logprobs.

Implemented by ExplanationNeuronSimulator.

class_variables.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.SimulationType.ONE_AT_A_TIME
documentation:
Use a separate prompt for each token being simulated; calculate EVs using logprobs.

Implemented by ExplanationTokenByTokenSimulator.
How to simulate neuron activations. Values correspond to subclasses of NeuronSimulator.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.NeuronSimulator

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.NeuronSimulator.simulate
documentation: Simulate the behavior of a neuron based on an explanation.
Abstract base class for simulating neuron behavior.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.ExplanationNeuronSimulator

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.ExplanationNeuronSimulator.make_simulation_prompt
documentation: Create a few-shot prompt for predicting neuron activations for the given tokens.
TODO(sbills): The current token<tab>activation format can result in improper tokenization.
In particular, if the token is itself a tab, we may get a single "\t\t" token rather than two
"\t" tokens. Consider using a separator that does not appear in any multi-character tokens.

Simulate neuron behavior based on an explanation.

This class uses a few-shot prompt with examples of other explanations and activations. This
prompt allows us to score all of the tokens at once using a nifty trick involving logprobs.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.ExplanationTokenByTokenSimulator

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.ExplanationTokenByTokenSimulator.make_single_token_simulation_prompt
documentation: Make a few-shot prompt for predicting the neuron's activation on a single token.

Simulate neuron behavior based on an explanation.

Unlike ExplanationNeuronSimulator, this class uses one few-shot prompt per token to calculate
expected activations. This is slower. This class gets a one-token completion and calculates an
expected value from that token's logprobs.

classes.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.LogprobFreeExplanationTokenSimulator

methods.name: neuron_explainer.explanations.simulator.LogprobFreeExplanationTokenSimulator._make_simulation_prompt
documentation: Make a few-shot prompt for predicting the neuron's activations on a sequence.

Simulate neuron behavior based on an explanation.

Unlike ExplanationNeuronSimulator and ExplanationTokenByTokenSimulator, this class does not rely on
logprobs to calculate expected activations. Instead, it uses a few-shot prompt that displays all of the
tokens at once, and request that the model repeat the tokens with the activations appended. Sampling
is with temperature = 0. Thus, the activations are deterministic. Also, each activation for a token
is a function of all the activations that came previously and all of the tokens in the sequence, not
just the current and previous tokens. In the case where the model does not faithfully reproduce the
token sequence, the simulator will return a response where every predicted activation is 0. Example prompt as follows:

Explanation: Explanation 1

Sequence 1 Tokens Without Activations:

A   _
B   _
C   _

Sequence 1 Tokens With Activations:

A   4_
B   10_
C   0_

Sequence 2 Tokens Without Activations:

D   _
E   _
F   _

Sequence 2 Tokens With Activations:

D   3_
E   6_
F   9_

Explanation: Explanation 2

Sequence 1 Tokens Without Activations:

G   _
H   _
I   _

Sequence 1 Tokens With Activations:
<start sampling here>

G   2_
H   0_
I   3_
